heading: Authority of Secretary to carry out certain functions and activities
label: 200103 Authority of Secretary to carry out certain functions and activities

L. 91–375, § 6(h)84 Stat. 776May 28, 1963Aug. 12, 1970In subsection (d), the words “which shall be prepared as soon as practicable within 5 years on and after ” and “Future” are omitted as obsolete.May 28, 1963In subsection (h), the word “department” is omitted as being included in “agency”.

Amendments2025—Subsecs. (d) to (i). redesignated subsecs. (e) to (i) as (d) to (h), respectively, and struck out former subsec. (d) which related to formulation and maintenance of a comprehensive nationwide outdoor recreation plan.Pub. L. 118–234

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: To carry out this chapter, the Secretary may perform the functions and activities described in this section.

children.heading: .—Inventory and Evaluation
children.label: b .—Inventory and Evaluation
children.text: The Secretary may prepare and maintain a continuing inventory and evaluation of outdoor recreation needs and resources of the United States.

children.heading: .—Classification System
children.label: c .—Classification System
children.text: The Secretary may prepare a system for classification of outdoor recreation resources to assist in the effective and beneficial use and management of such resources.

children.heading: .—Technical Assistance and Advice
children.label: d .—Technical Assistance and Advice
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance and advice to and cooperate with States, political subdivisions, and private interests, including nonprofit organizations, with respect to outdoor recreation.

children.heading: .—Interstate and Regional Cooperation
children.label: e .—Interstate and Regional Cooperation
children.text: The Secretary may encourage interstate and regional cooperation in the planning, acquisition, and development of outdoor recreation resources.

children.heading: .—Research, Information, and Education Programs and Activities
children.label: f .—Research, Information, and Education Programs and Activities

children.text: sponsor, engage in, and assist in research relating to outdoor recreation, directly or by contract or cooperative agreements, and make payments for such purposes without regard to the limitations of section 3324(a) and (b) of title 31 concerning advances of funds when the Secretary considers such action to be in the public interest;

undertake studies and assemble information concerning outdoor recreation, directly or by contract or cooperative agreement, and disseminate the information without regard to ; andsection 3204 of title 39
cooperate with educational institutions and others to assist in establishing education programs and activities and to encourage public use and benefits from outdoor recreation.

children.heading: Cooperation and Coordination with Federal Agencies.—
children.label: g Cooperation and Coordination with Federal Agencies.—
A
children.text: cooperate with and provide technical assistance to Federal agencies and obtain from them information, data, reports, advice, and assistance that are needed and can reasonably be furnished in carrying out the purposes of this chapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: promote coordination of Federal plans and activities generally relating to outdoor recreation.

children.heading: .—Funding
children.label: 2 .—Funding
children.text: An agency furnishing advice or assistance under this paragraph may expend its own funds for those purposes, with or without reimbursement, as may be agreed to by that agency.

children.heading: .—Donations
children.label: h .—Donations
children.text: The Secretary may accept and use donations of money, property, personal services, or facilities for the purposes of this chapter.
